title: Polypodiodes pseudoamoena
taxonomy: Polypodiodes pseudoamoena is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. It belongs to the kingdom Plantae and the phylum Pteridophyta. More specifically, it is classified within the division Pteridophyta, class Polypodiopsida, and order Polypodiales. The family Polypodiaceae is a group of vascular plants commonly known as the polypody ferns. The classification of Polypodiodes pseudoamoena reflects its evolutionary relationships with other fern species within this family.

It is often employed in the treatment of fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. The plant's extract is believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its medicinal uses. In traditional medicine, Polypodiodes pseudoamoena is also used to treat skin conditions, including eczema and dermatitis.
